articles.question: How do I create my first workspace?
articles.body: Open the Studio mini-app and click '+ New Workspace'. Pick a target jurisdiction (UKGC, MGA, NJ, etc.). The wizard creates an IR scaffold, default symbol set, and stub paytable so you can run a smoke spin in under a minute.

articles.question: What is included in the 30-day trial?
articles.body: The trial gives you 3 workspaces, 50 Monte Carlo runs per day, and 5 cert submissions per month. Community-tier support via the public KB and discussion forum. Upgrade to Pro for higher caps and 24h email SLA.

articles.question: Can I import an existing GDD?
articles.body: Yes. The IR importer accepts L&W's internal Excel format, the IGT XML schema, and a generic JSON. Drag the file onto the Studio canvas; the engine parses and validates against the schema in under 2 seconds for typical games.

articles.question: How accurate is the closed-form RTP?
articles.body: The closed-form solver matches Monte Carlo to within 1e-6 absolute RTP for all 77 kernel types in the test suite. Cluster and cascade kernels typically converge to 4 decimals with 100M MC spins.

articles.question: Which mechanics does the engine support?
articles.body: Lines, ways, cluster, Megaways, hold-and-spin, cascade with multipliers, mystery symbols, sticky wilds, walking wilds, big-bet packages, multi-tier wheels, must-hit jackpots, and 60+ more — see the master TODO and L&W M1-M16 portfolio coverage doc.

articles.question: Can I customize the volatility profile?
articles.body: Yes. Each kernel exposes parameters (paytable scale, hit-frequency target, bonus-trigger probability). The math optimizer can solve for a target volatility class (low/med/high/very-high) given an RTP constraint.

articles.question: How do I generate a PAR sheet?
articles.body: From the CERTIFY tab in Studio, click 'Generate PAR'. The engine emits a PDF with closed-form RTP, hit frequency, max-win cap, jackpot tier, jurisdictional gating flags, and an HSM-signed footer hash.

articles.question: Which labs accept the operator-package.zip?
articles.body: GLI, BMM, eCOGRA, NMi accept the bundle as a research input. UKGC requires a separate compliance statement; MGA and AGCO accept the full package as primary evidence. Always check with your specific lab account manager.

articles.question: How are jurisdictional gates enforced?
articles.body: Each game declares an allow-list of jurisdictions in its IR. The submission flow checks RTP bounds (e.g., UKGC requires >= 90%), max-win caps (Italy caps at 100x bet for slots), and feature restrictions (auto-play disabled for ON-AGCO).

articles.question: What is the HSM signature for?
articles.body: Every operator-package.zip is signed with an ed25519 key held by the HSM module. The signature lets the regulator independently verify the PAR + audit chain were not tampered with after generation. The public key ships with the dossier.

articles.question: How do I upgrade from Trial to Pro?
articles.body: Click 'My Account' in the Operator dashboard or use the Onboarding portal. Upgrade is immediate; caps and SLA apply from the timestamp of the upgrade API call. Prorated billing applies for mid-month upgrades.

articles.question: Can I pay annually?
articles.body: Yes. Annual Pro is $50K (~17% discount). Enterprise is $250K annual or custom multi-year deal — talk to sales. Invoices in USD, EUR, or GBP; net-30 terms.

articles.question: What happens when my trial expires?
articles.body: At day 25 you get a warning email. At day 31 the license locks: existing data is preserved, but you cannot run new MC sims or submit cert dossiers until you upgrade. Upgrade reactivates the account instantly.

articles.question: How do I connect my operator stack?
articles.body: Use the GaaS REST or WebSocket API. POST /api/session/start, then drive spins via /api/session/:id/spin. The /api/gaas/live WebSocket emits real-time spin events for monitoring.

articles.question: What auth does the GaaS API use?
articles.body: GAAS_API_KEYS environment variable holds a comma-separated list of accepted keys. Pass via 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' header. Per-tenant rate limits apply (default 600 req/min, configurable).

articles.question: Can I embed the game UI in my operator site?
articles.body: Yes. The /web/cabinet bundle is a self-contained iframe-able player. Configure CSP frame-ancestors to allow your operator domain. WebGL + Web Audio API required.

articles.question: How do I add team members?
articles.body: Open the Operator dashboard → Settings → Team. Invite by email; roles are admin / developer / viewer. RBAC enforced at the API layer; viewer cannot trigger MC runs or cert submissions.

articles.question: Can I export my data?
articles.body: Yes. From the Operator dashboard → My Account → Export, request a ZIP of all your IRs, MC outputs, and cert dossiers. Available within 24h. GDPR-compliant.

articles.question: How do I change my primary jurisdiction?
articles.body: Open the Operator dashboard → Settings → Account. The primary jurisdiction sets the default gating profile but does not restrict which jurisdictions individual games can target.

articles.question: What if I forget my password?
articles.body: Click 'Forgot password?' on the login screen. We send a reset link to your registered email; the link expires in 1 hour. Contact support@example.com if you cannot receive the email.

articles.question: Where can I find sample IR files?
articles.body: The /docs/research and /web/marketplace ship with sample IRs for Quick Hit Platinum, Huff n Puff, Megaways Vault, and Spartacus Colossal. Use them as a starting point for your first workspace.

articles.question: How long does a full MC validation take?
articles.body: A 100M-spin run on a base game with 5 reels takes ~12 seconds on an M2 Mac. Cluster + cascade kernels take ~40s. The optimizer runs in parallel via piscina workers; expect linear speedup up to 8 cores.

incidents.title: Brief 502 spikes on /api/cert/submit
incidents.summary: Cert worker queue saturated for 28 minutes after a load test. Capacity raised; auto-scaler thresholds tuned.

incidents.title: Marketplace search returning stale results
incidents.summary: ETag cache invalidation bug. Hotfix deployed; backfill complete.

incidents.title: HSM signing latency elevated
incidents.summary: Key-rotation drill ran longer than planned. Routine; no customer impact beyond +400ms PAR submission latency.
